revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Higashinomiya Kofun]][[ja:東之宮古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Inuyama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Higashinomiya Kofun | native_name =東之宮古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Higashinomiya Kofun (1), Kitahakusambira Inuyama Inuyama City 2022.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Higashinomiya Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Higashinomiya Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.39|N|136.950278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Inuyama|qid=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Higashinomiya Kofun'''|東之宮古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Inuyama, Aichi|en|Inuyama, Aichi|qid=Q503411|lt=Inuyama, Aichi|1=Inuyama, Aichi}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|qid=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138544|title=東之宮古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Higashinomiya Kofun is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), located at the northwest end of the Aki Hills on the left bank of the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|qid=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}} in the northeast of Inuyama city. Built by cutting away a natural hill with an elevation of 143 meters, it has a typical keyhole-shaped form and a view of the Owari Plain. The tumulus is 72 meters long, with a posterior portion 48 x 49 meters with an eight-meter height, and an anterior portion of 43 x 43 meters, with a six-meter height. It was orientated facing northwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus has two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one in the posterior and one in the anterior portion, both parallel to the main axis of the Kofun. Since the posterior portion was broken into and partially destroyed by grave robbers, an emergency [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted by the Inuyama City Board of Education in 1973. A vertical-type stone chamber with a gravel floor was found, with the surfaces of the walls and ceiling covered in clay and painted in vermillion. Many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were recovered, including eleven ancient [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical beads, jade balls, wheel-shaped stones, iron implements (including swords, daggers, spears and agricultural implements) and a cracked bamboo-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. From these grave goods, it was estimated that the ''kofun'' dates from the late 3rd to early 4th century, making it one of the earliest in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}. All of the excavated items were designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1978<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/135094|title=尾張東之宮古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=}}</ref> and are now stored at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|qid=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum|1=Kyoto National Museum}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
From 2005 to 2007, another excavation survey was carried out by the Inuyama City Board of Education for historic site maintenance, and it was determined that the mound was constructed in two tiers and was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. The total length was extended to 78 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The ''kofun'' is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Inuyama Yuen Station|Inuyama Yuen Station]] on the {{ill|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|en|Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|qid=Q6810415|lt=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line|1=Meitetsu Kakamigahara Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|qid=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline}} *[https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kunisitei/0875.html Aichi Cultural Properties Navi] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.inuyama.aichi.jp/kurashi/bunka/1003488/1003981/1003511.html Inuyama City official site] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Authority control}}
Higashinomiya Kofun
